reforms.description: The government has increased resources for lifelong learning, allocating around GBP 2 billion to the National Skills Fund to support free courses in areas with skill shortages, such as healthcare and digital technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skill set of the workforce, addressing skill shortages and improving productivity in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated funding to support free courses through the National Skills Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant investment in workforce development that targets skill shortages, with potential long-term benefits for productivity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has increased resources for lifelong learning, allocating around GBP 2 billion to the National Skills Fund in the period up to 2024/25.

reforms.description: The UK government announced a two-year tax 'super-deduction' allowing companies to claim a 130% capital allowance on qualifying plant and machinery investments, aimed at boosting business investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages firms to invest in new assets, thereby enhancing productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The super-deduction was introduced in the March 2021 budget and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The super-deduction represents a significant incentive for business investment, which is crucial for economic recovery post-pandemic.
reforms.source_quote: To support business investment, the government announced a two-year tax 'super-deduction' in the March 2021 budget.

reforms.description: The UK government has committed to increasing public investment in R&D to GBP 20 billion a year by FY24/25, aiming to raise overall R&D spending to GBP 22 billion by 2026/27 and achieve an economy-wide R&D investment of 2.4% of GDP by 2027.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced its commitment to increase public investment in R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public R&D funding, which is crucial for driving innovation and addressing long-term economic challenges.
reforms.source_quote: Increase public investment in R&D to GBP 20 billion a year by FY24/25, part of the government’s objectives to increase R&D spending to GBP 22 billion by 2026/27.

reforms.description: The UK government introduced the GBP 375 million 'Future Fund: Breakthrough' to address the scale-up gap for innovative businesses and other equity products, aiming to support high-growth firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to innovative startups, the fund aims to enhance their growth potential and contribute to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was introduced to support innovative businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant investment aimed at addressing a critical gap in the startup ecosystem, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Introduced GBP 375 million 'Future Fund: Breakthrough' to address the scale up gap for innovative businesses and other equity products.

reforms.description: The UK government announced an increase in public R&D investment to GBP 20 billion by 2024-25, aiming to stimulate private investment and raise overall R&D spending to 2.4% of GDP by 2026-27.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to leverage additional private investment, enhancing overall innovation capacity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced to increase public R&D investment to GBP 20 billion by 2024-25.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, which is crucial for enhancing the UK's innovation landscape and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced to increase public R&D investment to GBP 20 billion by 2024-25.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to broaden the scope of qualifying expenditure for R&D tax credits to include data and cloud computing from April 2023, enhancing support for data-driven research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By expanding the R&D tax credit to cover data and cloud computing, the reform is expected to incentivize more firms to invest in innovative research and development.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced to broaden the scope of qualifying expenditure for R&D tax credits to include data and cloud computing from April 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the existing R&D tax credit framework, making it more relevant to current technological advancements and encouraging investment in innovative sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The scope of qualifying expenditure for the R&D tax credit was announced to be broadened to include data and cloud computing.

reforms.description: The UK government aims to install 600,000 heat pumps per year by 2028 to support the transition to net zero, addressing the need for substantial training of heat pump installers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to build a skilled workforce necessary for the green transition, which is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to install 600,000 heat pumps per year by 2028.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to address skill shortages in the green economy, which is crucial for achieving net zero targets.
reforms.source_quote: To meet the net zero target, the government aims to install 600,000 heat pumps per year by 2028.

reforms.description: The United Kingdom is considering expanding the scope of the UK Emissions Trading Scheme (ETS) to include additional sectors such as domestic shipping and energy-from-waste, which would enhance policy efficiency and reduce emissions volatility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding the ETS coverage can lead to more comprehensive emissions reductions, fostering innovation in low-carbon technologies across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is calling for evidence on expanding the scope of the ETS.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing climate policy, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of emissions pricing and support the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.source_quote: The United Kingdom is considering expanding the scope of the UK ETS in existing sectors, such as: calling for evidence on the inclusion of upstream oil and gas venting sectors, and to sectors currently not subject to an explicit carbon price.

reforms.description: The UK government has committed to a significant expansion of renewable electricity capacity, aiming to add 50 GW of offshore wind and 24 GW of nuclear capacity by 2050, supported by the Contract for Difference (CfD) scheme.
reforms.component_name: Offshore Wind and Nuclear Capacity Expansion
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This expansion is expected to enhance energy security and drive innovation in clean energy technologies, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched initiatives to support the expansion of renewable energy capacity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the UK's energy policy, aiming to significantly increase renewable energy production and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
reforms.source_quote: The government has an ambition to add notably 50 GW of offshore wind and 24 GW additional nuclear capacity.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to develop up to ten gigawatts of low-carbon hydrogen production capacity by 2030, supported by a GBP 240 million Net Zero Hydrogen Fund to provide capital and development subsidies for new hydrogen production facilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to enhance the UK's energy transition by promoting low-carbon technologies, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to develop hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in low-carbon technology, which is crucial for the UK's energy transition and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The United Kingdom plans to develop up to ten gigawatts of low-carbon hydrogen production capacity by 2030.

reforms.description: The UK government is providing grants through the Boiler Upgrade Scheme to support homeowners in installing heat pumps, with grants of GBP 5,000 available from April 2022.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme encourages the adoption of cleaner heating technologies, which can lead to reduced emissions and stimulate innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced grants for heat pumps to begin in April 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant step towards promoting cleaner heating solutions, contributing to the UK's climate goals and supporting innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in its Heat and Building Strategy to give homeowners grants of GBP 5,000 to install heat pumps from April 2022.

reforms.description: The UK government is reviewing agricultural support to increasingly reward climate and ecosystem services, alongside direct investment in research and development to encourage innovative low carbon farming techniques.
reforms.component_name: Support for Low Carbon Farming Techniques
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging innovative practices in agriculture can lead to more sustainable production methods, enhancing productivity and reducing emissions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is reviewing agricultural support to tilt payments towards rewarding climate and ecosystem services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in agricultural policy towards sustainability and innovation, with potential long-term benefits for the sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government is reviewing agricultural support to increasingly tilt payments towards rewarding climate and ecosystem services and encouraging low-emitting production methods.

reforms.description: The UK government is exploring a hybrid system for agricultural emissions that combines quantity-based measures with a carbon price, while also subsidizing improved practices and green R&D through competitive auction designs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to enhance productivity and sustainability in agriculture, potentially leading to increased efficiency and reduced emissions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is consulting on measuring and reporting agricultural emissions as part of this reform.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how agricultural emissions are managed, integrating market mechanisms with R&D support.
reforms.source_quote: A hybrid system, in which quantity-based measures are subject to a carbon price, while improved practices, the provision of nature-based services and green R&D are subsidised, preferably in competitive auction designs, would likely increase policy efficiency and should be explored.

reforms.description: The UK government is committing GBP 750 million from the Nature for Climate Fund to support tree planting and peatland restoration efforts, which are essential for carbon sequestration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in natural capital enhances ecosystem services and contributes to climate goals, which can lead to long-term economic benefits.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is committing GBP 750 million funding from the Nature for Climate Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a significant investment in environmental sustainability and climate resilience, with potential long-term economic impacts.
reforms.source_quote: The England Trees Action Plan committed to more than doubling tree planting to 30 000 hectares per year, while the England Peat Action Plan sets out to restore 35 000 hectares of peatland by 2025.
